name: Sevilleja de la Jara and La Nava de Ricomalillo Loop from El Campillo de la Jara
name_original: Sevilleja de la Jara y La Nava de Ricomalillo – circular desde El Campillo de la Jara
description: This moderate 26.1-mile road cycling route from El Campillo de la Jara explores the natural beauty of Toledo province.
region_intro: Road cycling around El Campillo De La Jara is characterized by its rural landscapes and varied terrain, offering routes that traverse rolling hills and open countryside. The region features a network of roads suitable for cycling, with some routes incorporating passes that indicate more significant elevation changes. This area provides opportunities for road cyclists to explore the natural environment of the Comarca de la Jara. Best road cycling routes around El Campillo De La Jara The most popular road cycling route is Comarca de la Jara – circular to Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé desde El Campillo de la Jara, a 38.4 miles (61.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through the local rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sevilleja de la Jara and La Nava de Ricomalillo Loop from El Campillo de la Jara, a moderate 26.1 miles (42.0 km) path. This loop route provides varied terrain and scenic views of the surrounding area. Local road cyclists also love the San Vicente Pass – Guadalupe Viaduct loop from El Campillo de la Jara, a 61.8 miles (99.4 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around El Campillo De La Jara is defined by rural roads, rolling hills, and mountain passes. The network offers options for moderate and challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Campillo De La Jara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around El Campillo De La Jara?
A: There are 17 road cycling routes documented on komoot for the El Campillo De La Jara area. These routes traverse rural landscapes, rolling hills, and some mountain passes, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in El Campillo De La Jara?
A: While there are no routes specifically graded as 'easy', many of the 13 moderate routes can be suitable for road cycling beginners looking for a gentle challenge. These routes typically feature varied terrain without extreme elevation gains, allowing for a comfortable introduction to the region's cycling. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025297653/comarca-de-la-jara-circular-to-aldeanueva-de-san-bartolome-desde-el-campillo-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comarca de la Jara – circular to Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé desde El Campillo de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes near El Campillo De La Jara?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers 4 difficult routes. These routes often include significant elevation gains and mountain passes. A notable challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Pass – Guadalupe Viaduct loop from El Campillo de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 100 km with substantial climbing.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes in the region?
A: If you're looking for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Pass – Guadalupe Viaduct loop from El Campillo de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longest, stretching almost 100 km. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Pass – Guadalupe Viaduct loop from Puerto de San Vicente&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 71 km long and features considerable elevation.

Q: Are there many circular road cycling routes around El Campillo De La Jara?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in El Campillo De La Jara are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025268408/sevilleja-de-la-jara-and-la-nava-de-ricomalillo-loop-from-el-campillo-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sevilleja de la Jara and La Nava de Ricomalillo Loop from El Campillo de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; and the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Campillo de la Jara loop from El Campillo de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What scenic attractions or landmarks can I see while road cycling in El Campillo De La Jara?
A: The region offers several points of interest along its routes. You might encounter historical railway structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4253513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Quiteria Station&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4632240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Campillo-Sevilleja railway station&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2606458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Pass&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3853975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huso River Valley and Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in El Campillo De La Jara?
A: El Campillo De La Jara, located in a rural area, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the middle of the day, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months. Winters are typically mild but can have cooler, wetter days.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in El Campillo De La Jara?
A: While the routes are primarily graded moderate to difficult, families with older children or those comfortable with longer distances might enjoy some of the moderate loop routes. The region's rural roads generally have less traffic, which can be a plus for family rides. Consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025297653/comarca-de-la-jara-circular-to-aldeanueva-de-san-bartolome-desde-el-campillo-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comarca de la Jara – circular to Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé desde El Campillo de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; for a moderate family outing.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around El Campillo De La Jara?
A: Road cycling around El Campillo De La Jara is characterized by a mix of rural roads, rolling hills, and some mountain passes. You'll experience varied terrain, from open countryside to areas with more significant elevation changes, particularly on the difficult routes. The region offers a good balance for cyclists who enjoy both steady pedaling and challenging climbs.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in El Campillo De La Jara?
A: Over 80 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Campillo De La Jara. Users frequently appreciate the region's varied terrain, which offers both moderate and challenging options, and the opportunity to experience the natural environment of the Comarca de la Jara away from heavy traffic. The scenic views and rural charm are often highlighted.

Q: Are there specific routes that incorporate the Vía Verde de la Jara?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2552694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; is a notable cycle path in the area, converted from an old railway line. While primarily a gravel path, some road cycling routes may run parallel to or offer access points to this scenic trail, allowing for a different cycling experience or a break from paved roads. Check individual route details for specific intersections.
tips: La Nava de Ricomalilo is a small town in the region of La Jara surrounded by a beautiful countryside of olive groves and private orchards at the foot of a hill called the Risco de La Palma. The environment is very pleasant and in its urban center is the curious stone and brick tower of the town hall. The town has various services ideal for a stop on the way.

As its name indicates, El Campillo de la Jara is an eminently agricultural enclave surrounded by cereal fields. Formerly, it had several grain mills. The town is one of the many through which the La Jara greenway passes.

faqs.question: How much time should I allocate for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 42 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 9 minutes to complete by racebike. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling speed and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as moderate. While it involves some elevation changes (around 500 meters of ascent), it is manageable for regular road cyclists with a good fitness level. Absolute beginners might find it challenging, but those accustomed to road cycling should be fine.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily characterized by firm pavement, making it well-suited for racebikes. However, as it incorporates sections of the Vía Verde de la Jara, be aware that some segments might have variations in surface quality, potentially including tamped gravel or compacted earth, though the majority should be paved.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll pass through charming settlements like El Campillo de la Jara and can admire the Town Hall Clock Tower of La Nava de Ricomalillo. The route also utilizes sections of the historic Vía Verde de la Jara, featuring old railway stations, impressive viaducts, and tunnels. Remember to bring a flashlight for the tunnels, even if most are lit.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and nature will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the beautiful comarca of La Jara, known for its natural landscapes. Expect picturesque views of pine, oak, and cork oak forests, as well as vibrant fields of rockrose, especially in spring. The route offers glimpses of the Tagus River Valley, the San Vicente Pass, and the Huso River Valley, with some sections near the San Vicente Reservoir.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring is particularly recommended for cycling this route, as the fields of rockrose are in full bloom, adding to the visual appeal. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for cycling in this region, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Where does this cycling route begin and where can I park?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route that starts and ends in El Campillo de la Jara. You should be able to find parking facilities within the village itself, as it serves as a common starting point for local routes.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily on paved roads and sections of the Vía Verde, which are generally accessible, it's a road cycling route. If you plan to bring a dog, it would need to be able to keep pace with a bicycle or be carried in a suitable trailer. Always ensure your dog is on a leash and that you clean up after it.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It uses public roads and sections of the Vía Verde de la Jara, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths, including the Vía Verde de la Jara, Camino de los Montes de Toledo - Etapa 7, Camino de la Jara - Etapa 3, and Camino de Buenasbodas o de Guadalupe por los Montes de Toledo.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for this route?
faqs.answer: As parts of the route utilize the Vía Verde de la Jara, which has historically seen temporary closures due to safety concerns with embankments and tunnels, it's advisable to check the current status of the Vía Verde before your trip to ensure all sections are accessible and safe for cycling. Always carry a flashlight for the tunnels.

waypoints.name: Town Hall Clock Tower of La Nava de Ricomalillo
